title: User.status enum
description: The four terminal states a User.status column may hold. Transitions between states are documented in `events.json` (each transition fires a distinct lifecycle event). Business-type + tenant setting layers may skip states (e.g. bypass `pending` when the tenant sets `requires_admin_approval = false` AND the Identity's email is already verified), but the physical enum is stable.
column_note: Persisted as a string(24). Cast to the `UserStatus` enum on the model.

statuses.description: Newly provisioned. Cannot authenticate. Transitions to `active` when (a) the underlying Identity's email is verified AND (b) `user.registration.requires_admin_approval` is false, OR when an admin explicitly activates.
The normal operational state. Can authenticate, participate in TenantMember memberships, receive notifications.
Admin-invoked pause. Sanctum PATs are revoked but the User row survives. Distinguished from `disabled` in intent — suspension is temporary + expected to reverse; disabled is terminal.
Terminal state short of erasure. Row survives (for audit + retention) but the User cannot authenticate or be reactivated. Erasure (soft-delete + PurgeErasedUsersJob) is the final step past this.
